tasks.objective: Maintain a current safety plan as the kitchen state changes.

turns.content: A robot is in a kitchen. The floor is dry. The stove is on. A child is nearby. What should it track?
The child leaves. The floor becomes wet. The stove turns off. The robot is now carrying hot water.
What should the robot do next, and which facts no longer matter?

retain: robot is now carrying hot water

tasks.objective: Resume an experimental design after an unrelated interruption.

turns.content: Design an experiment to test whether blue light affects seedling growth. Start by identifying hypothesis, variables, controls, and the next unresolved design choice.
Interruption: summarize the difference between a comet and an asteroid in one sentence.
Return to the experiment. Continue from the unresolved design choice without restarting.

tasks.objective: Reanalyze a contract scenario after replacing an earlier legal assumption.

turns.content: Analyze a vendor contract assuming the limitation-of-liability clause is enforceable. Identify likely damages exposure and negotiation posture.
Now return to the point before that assumption. Assume the limitation-of-liability clause is unenforceable. Redo the analysis from there.

tasks.objective: Preserve three partial findings and combine them into a unified mechanism.

turns.content: Subproblem A: A patient cohort shows elevated IL-6 after treatment but no fever. Save the important partial finding.
Subproblem B: The same cohort shows reduced mitochondrial spare respiratory capacity. Save the important partial finding separately.
Subproblem C: Symptoms improve when the treatment is paired with a mild AMPK activator. Save the important partial finding separately.
Now braid the three partial findings into one plausible mechanism. Do not force unrelated details together.
